---
title: "occ_sanitychecks"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-01"
---

# cleaning up occurrence data by taxon

## load packages

```{julia}
include("helper_fxns.jl")
```

## load in files

```{julia}
taxa_traits = DataFrame(CSV.File("data/taxonomy_trait_data/traits_species_cleaned-2026_02_25.csv"))
homonyms = DataFrame(CSV.File("data/taxonomy_trait_data/homonyms.csv"))
taxa_list = taxa_traits.scientificName
```

## reconcile georeferenced file + clean

```{julia}
for (idx, taxa) in enumerate(taxa_list)
    filename = replace(taxa, " " => "_")
    taxon_stem = replace(filename, r"-\d{4}_\d{2}.*$" => "")
    georef_dir = "data/occurrence_data/pt_occs_georeferenced"
    georef_df = nothing
    for ext in [".csv", ".xlsx"]
        # Match any file starting with the taxon filename in the georef directory
        georef_candidates = filter(
            f -> startswith(f, filename) && endswith(f, ext),
            readdir(georef_dir)
        )
        if !isempty(georef_candidates)
            georef_path = joinpath(georef_dir, first(georef_candidates))
            println("  Found georeferenced file: $georef_path")
            try
                if ext == ".csv"
                    georef_df = DataFrame(CSV.File(georef_path))
                else
                    # Read xlsx via R
                    @rput georef_path
                    R"georef_r <- readxl::read_excel(georef_path)"
                    @rget georef_r
                    georef_df = georef_r
                end
                # Normalize Viable column: strip whitespace, uppercase, filter TRUE
                viable_col = findfirst(c -> uppercase(string(c)) == "VIABLE", names(georef_df))
                if !isnothing(viable_col)
                    rename!(georef_df, names(georef_df)[viable_col] => :Viable)
                    georef_df[!, :Viable] = map(v -> uppercase(strip(string(v))), georef_df.Viable)
                    georef_df = filter(row -> row.Viable == "TRUE", georef_df)
                    # Coerce lat/lon to Float64 (CSV may read them as strings)
                    georef_df[!, :latitude] = map(v -> ismissing(v) ? missing : tryparse(Float64, string(v)), georef_df.latitude)
                    georef_df[!, :longitude] = map(v -> ismissing(v) ? missing : tryparse(Float64, string(v)), georef_df.longitude)
                    # Keep only rows with valid numeric coordinates
                    georef_df = filter(row -> !ismissing(row.latitude) && !ismissing(row.longitude), georef_df)
                    println("  Georeferenced viable points: $(nrow(georef_df))")
                else
                    println("  WARNING: No 'Viable' column found in $georef_path — skipping georef points")
                    georef_df = nothing
                end
            catch e
                println("  WARNING: Could not read georef file $georef_path: $e")
                georef_df = nothing
            end
            break

        end #is not empty
    end

    if !isnothing(georef_df) && nrow(georef_df) > 0
        # Find the no_coords file — this is the DwC base for georef records
        no_coords_candidates = filter(
            f -> startswith(f, taxon_stem) && endswith(f, "_no_coords.csv"),
            readdir("data/occurrence_data/pt_occs_to_georeference")
        )

        # Find the cleaned file — records that already had coordinates
        clean_candidates = filter(
            f -> startswith(f, taxon_stem) && occursin("cleaned", f) && endswith(f, ".csv"),
            readdir("data/occurrence_data/pt_occs_clean")
        )

        if isempty(no_coords_candidates)
            println("  WARNING: No no_coords file found for $taxon_stem — skipping georef merge")
        elseif isempty(clean_candidates)
            println("  WARNING: No cleaned file found for $taxon_stem — skipping georef merge")
        else
            no_coords_path = joinpath("data/occurrence_data/pt_occs_to_georeference", first(no_coords_candidates))
            no_coords_df = DataFrame(CSV.File(no_coords_path))

            clean_path = joinpath("data/occurrence_data/pt_occs_clean", first(clean_candidates))
            clean_df = DataFrame(CSV.File(clean_path))

            # Filter no_coords to only viable georef IDs
            georef_ids = Set(georef_df.ID)
            georef_rows = filter(row -> string(row.ID) in Set(string.(collect(georef_ids))), no_coords_df)

            if nrow(georef_rows) == 0
                println("  WARNING: No overlapping IDs between georef and no_coords file")
            else
                # Replace lat/lon with georeferenced coordinates
                georef_lookup = Dict(
                    string(row.ID) => (lat=row.latitude, lon=row.longitude)
                    for row in eachrow(georef_df)
                )
                georef_rows[!, :latitude] = map(row -> georef_lookup[string(row.ID)].lat, eachrow(georef_rows))
                georef_rows[!, :longitude] = map(row -> georef_lookup[string(row.ID)].lon, eachrow(georef_rows))

                # Add georef notes column to both frames so vcat works
                notes_lookup = Dict(
                    string(row.ID) => get(row, :Notes, missing)
                    for row in eachrow(georef_df)
                )
                georef_rows[!, :georef_notes] = map(row -> get(notes_lookup, string(row.ID), missing), eachrow(georef_rows))
                clean_df[!, :georef_notes] = Vector{Union{Missing,String}}(missing, nrow(clean_df))

                # Combine: existing coords records + newly georeferenced records
                combined = vcat(clean_df, georef_rows, cols=:union)

                println("  Combined: $(nrow(clean_df)) clean + $(nrow(georef_rows)) georef = $(nrow(combined)) total")

                out_path = joinpath("data/occurrence_data/pt_occs_clean", taxon_stem * "_georef_merged.csv")
                CSV.write(out_path, combined; transform=(col, val) -> something(val, missing))
                println("  Written: $out_path")
            end
        end

    else
        println("  No viable georeferenced points — using cleaned file as-is")
    end
end
```

## set up cleaning functions

notably: these functions edit the file in-place (georef_merged preferred over cleaned).

```{julia}
# Shared helper: resolve the best available CSV path for a taxon
function resolve_taxon_path(taxon::String)
    clean_dir = "data/occurrence_data/pt_occs_clean"
    taxon_stem = replace(taxon, " " => "_")

    georef_candidates = filter(
        f -> startswith(f, taxon_stem) && endswith(f, "_georef_merged.csv"),
        readdir(clean_dir)
    )
    cleaned_candidates = filter(
        f -> startswith(f, taxon_stem) && occursin("cleaned", f) && endswith(f, ".csv"),
        readdir(clean_dir)
    )

    if !isempty(georef_candidates)
        path = joinpath(clean_dir, first(georef_candidates))
        println("  Using georef_merged file: $path")
        return path
    elseif !isempty(cleaned_candidates)
        path = joinpath(clean_dir, first(cleaned_candidates))
        println("  Using cleaned file (no georef_merged found): $path")
        return path
    else
        error("No cleaned or georef_merged file found for taxon: $taxon")
    end
end

function filter_countries(taxon::String, countries::Vector{String})
    path = resolve_taxon_path(taxon)
    cleandf = DataFrame(CSV.File(path))

    # check that at least one country is present in df
    dfcountries = cleandf.country
    if length(intersect(countries, dfcountries)) == 0
        error("no input country provided in `countries` list matches countries existing in dataframe")
    end

    # filter every country out of df
    for country in countries
        filter!(:country => x -> x != country, cleandf)
    end
    CSV.write(path, cleandf)
end

# Remove rows outside the specified lat/lon bounding box.
# All bounds are optional — specify only the axes you want to constrain.
# Points with missing coordinates are dropped when a bound is supplied for that axis.
# Always plots a preview map (green = kept, red × = removed).
# Set save=true to write the filtered result back to the file.
function filter_coords(taxon::String;
    lat_min=nothing, lat_max=nothing,
    lon_min=nothing, lon_max=nothing,
    save::Bool=false)

    if all(isnothing, (lat_min, lat_max, lon_min, lon_max))
        error("At least one of lat_min, lat_max, lon_min, lon_max must be specified")
    end

    path = resolve_taxon_path(taxon)
    df = DataFrame(CSV.File(path))

    # Build a row-wise keep predicate
    function keep(row)
        lat = row.latitude
        lon = row.longitude
        if !isnothing(lat_min) || !isnothing(lat_max)
            (ismissing(lat) || !isa(lat, Number)) && return false
            !isnothing(lat_min) && lat < lat_min && return false
            !isnothing(lat_max) && lat > lat_max && return false
        end
        if !isnothing(lon_min) || !isnothing(lon_max)
            (ismissing(lon) || !isa(lon, Number)) && return false
            !isnothing(lon_min) && lon < lon_min && return false
            !isnothing(lon_max) && lon > lon_max && return false
        end
        return true
    end

    keep_mask = [keep(row) for row in eachrow(df)]
    kept_df = df[keep_mask, :]
    removed_df = df[.!keep_mask, :]
    n_kept = nrow(kept_df)
    n_removed = nrow(removed_df)

    println("  Preview: $n_removed point(s) will be removed, $n_kept will be kept")

    # Plot preview: green = kept, red × = removed
    taxon_label = taxon
    kept_lat = Vector{Union{Missing,Float64}}(kept_df.latitude)
    kept_lon = Vector{Union{Missing,Float64}}(kept_df.longitude)
    removed_lat = Vector{Union{Missing,Float64}}(removed_df.latitude)
    removed_lon = Vector{Union{Missing,Float64}}(removed_df.longitude)
    @rput taxon_label kept_lat kept_lon removed_lat removed_lon
    R"""
    kept_pts    <- data.frame(latitude  = as.numeric(kept_lat),
                              longitude = as.numeric(kept_lon))
    removed_pts <- data.frame(latitude  = as.numeric(removed_lat),
                              longitude = as.numeric(removed_lon))
    all_lat <- c(kept_pts$latitude,  removed_pts$latitude)
    all_lon <- c(kept_pts$longitude, removed_pts$longitude)
    world    <- annotation_borders(database="world", colour="gray80", fill="gray80")
    borders  <- annotation_borders(database="world", colour="gray40", fill=NA, size=0.5)
    p <- ggplot() + world + borders +
        geom_point(data=kept_pts,
                   aes(x=longitude, y=latitude),
                   color="darkgreen", size=2, alpha=0.7) +
        { if (nrow(removed_pts) > 0)
              geom_point(data=removed_pts,
                         aes(x=longitude, y=latitude),
                         color="red", shape=4, size=3.5, stroke=1.2, alpha=0.9)
          else NULL } +
        coord_sf(xlim=c(min(all_lon, na.rm=TRUE)-2, max(all_lon, na.rm=TRUE)+2),
                 ylim=c(min(all_lat, na.rm=TRUE)-2, max(all_lat, na.rm=TRUE)+2)) +
        labs(title=paste0(taxon_label, "  —  green: kept (", nrow(kept_pts),
                          ")   red ×: removed (", nrow(removed_pts), ")"),
             x="Longitude", y="Latitude") +
        theme_minimal() +
        theme(plot.title=element_text(size=10))
    print(p)
    """

    if save
        CSV.write(path, kept_df)
        println("  Written: $path")
    else
        println("  Preview only — re-run with save=true to apply.")
    end
end
```

## remove obs that aren't iNat or herbarium samples

```{julia}
# let's combine all the different types of preserved specimen string
replace!(all_occs.basisOfRecord, "preservedspecimen" => "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN")
replace!(all_occs.basisOfRecord, "PreservedSpecimen" => "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN")
replace!(all_occs.basisOfRecord, "Preserved Specimen" => "PRESERVED_SPECIMEN")

material_citations = filter(row -> row.basisOfRecord == "MATERIAL_CITATION", all_occs) # this is just one row for ascendens subsp. ascendens, and shouldn't be in this taxon anyway, it's actually a valid new taxon: nivalis subsp. lanatifolia
material_samples = filter(row -> row.basisOfRecord == "MATERIAL_SAMPLE", all_occs) # these come from GenBank --> need to be filtered
living_specimens = filter(row -> row.basisOfRecord == "LIVING_SPECIMEN", all_occs) # all from RBG Edinburgh
fossils = filter(row -> row.basisOfRecord == "FOSSIL_SPECIMEN", all_occs) # these are not actually fossils --> UC Berkeley garden specimens
filter!(row -> row[:basisOfRecord] != "MATERIAL_SAMPLE", all_occs)
filter!(row -> row[:basisOfRecord] != "MATERIAL_CITATION", all_occs)
filter!(row -> row[:basisOfRecord] != "LIVING_SPECIMEN", all_occs)
filter!(row -> row[:basisOfRecord] != "FOSSIL_SPECIMEN", all_occs)
```

```{julia}
# load mega df (georef_merged preferred over cleaned per taxon)
all_occs = load_pt_occs_df()
nrow(all_occs) #6757

# total points per taxon
n_by_taxon = combine(groupby(all_occs, :taxon), nrow => :n_total)
sort!(n_by_taxon, :n_total)
low_n_taxa = filter((:n_total => f -> f < 15), n_by_taxon)
```

```{julia}
n_by_taxon_min15 = filter((:n_total => f -> f >= 15), n_by_taxon)
filter!(row -> row[:taxon] in n_by_taxon_min15.taxon, all_occs)
nrow(all_occs) #6433
```

```{julia}
# points by taxon × basisOfRecord
n_by_bor = combine(groupby(all_occs, :taxon),
    :basisOfRecord => (b -> count(==("HUMAN_OBSERVATION"), b)) => :HUMAN_OBSERVATION,
    :basisOfRecord => (b -> count(==("PRESERVED_SPECIMEN"), b)) => :PRESERVED_SPECIMEN,
    nrow => :total_points
)

n_by_bor
```

## clean up specific taxa

problems observed in maps:
- integrifolia - this manuscript defines / carves out several species from integrifolia s.l.,
    defining integrifolia s.s. might be a problem if all these occurrences haven't been annotated / revisited post 2005.
    but Peru can definitely be removed.
```{julia}
filter_countries("Calceolaria integrifolia", ["Peru"])
```

- ascendens subsp. glandulifera
    - fixit: deal with nivalis (get rid of all, but essentially deal with synonym problem)
- ascendens subsp. ascendens

- polifolia - remove two -35 points
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria polifolia", lat_min=-35, save=true)
```


- nitida - can remove points -31 and above and -35 and below
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria nitida", lat_max=-31, lat_min=-34.5, save=true)
```

- cana - need to remove the Mexican point
```{julia}
filter_countries("Calceolaria cana", ["Mexico"])
```

- purpurea - remove point above -30 and below -35
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria purpurea", lat_max=-30, lat_min=-35, save=true)
```

- polyclada - remove point above -17
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria polyclada", lat_max=-17, save=true)
``` 

- teucrioides -remove point above -20
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria teucrioides", lat_max=-20, save=true)
```

- meyeniana subsp. meyeniana - one non georeferenced point that's out of range at -31
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria meyeniana subsp. meyeniana", lat_max=-31, save=true)
```

- corymbosa subsp. corymbosa
- corymbosa subsp. montana 

- glandulosa subsp. glandulosa
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria glandulosa subsp. glandulosa", lat_max=-28, lat_min=-39.6, save=true)
```

- crenatiflora - remove point above -15
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria crenatiflora", lat_max=-15, save=true)
```

- filicaulis subsp. filicaulis - remove points below -46 and above -32. 
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria filicaulis subsp. filicaulis", lat_min=-46, lat_max=-32, save=true)
```

-filicaulis subsp. luxurians - remove points above -30
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria filicaulis subsp. luxurians", lat_max=-30, save=true)
```

- uniflora -remove points above -45
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria uniflora", lat_max=-45, save=true)
```

- biflora

- rupestris - -9 point out of range as described by Molau (not in Junin, Huancavelica, or Ayacucho)
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria rupestris", lat_max=-10, save=true)
```

- undulata - remove one -39 point
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria undulata", lat_min=-39, save=true)
```

- tenella - remove point above -36
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria tenella", lat_max=-36, save=true)
```

- picta - any point below -32 does not fit into range
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria picta", lat_min=-32, save=true)
```

- flavovirens - remove -29 point
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria flavovirens", lat_max=-29, save=true)
```

- hypericina - remove -45 point and -72 point thats on the coast, endemic to andes
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria hypericina", lat_min=-45, lon_min=-71.3, save=true)
```

- cavanillesii - remove points below -39, outside of regions VII- IX range
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria cavanillesii", lat_min=-39, save=true)
```

- cypripediiflora - remove points above -12, outside of district of Puno and Cuzco as described by Molau
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria cypripediiflora", lat_max=-12, save=true)
```

- dentata subsp. dentata - can remove the point in Argentina
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria dentata subsp. dentata", lon_max=-68, save=true)
```

- speciosa - remove -16 point
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria speciosa", lat_min=-16, save=true)
```

- valdiviana subsp. valdiviana - remove one -43 point
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria valdiviana subsp. valdiviana", lat_min=-43, save=true)
```

- linearis - remove nongeoref (-8, -74) pt
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria linearis", lon_max=-74.2, save=true)
```

- myriophylla - remove one nongeoref -8 pt
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria myriophylla", lat_max=-9, lon_min=-74, save=true)
```

- inflexa - Molau says endemic to Peru. I don't know if I believe the two Bolivian points.
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria inflexa", lon_max=-70, save=true)
```

- umbellata - fixit: delete the -35ºS pt, it comes from GenBank.
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria umbellata", lat_min=-35, save=true)
```

- angustiflora - remove Chilean points. 1 of the southern Peru pts included in Molau monograph.
```{julia}
filter_countries("Calceolaria angustiflora", ["Chile"])
```

- chrysophaera - two points in Bolivia, which doesn't agree with Molau. but those two were det. by Puppo, who I trust.

- paposana - remove points above -23 and below -30
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria paposana", lat_max=-23, lat_min=-30, save=true)
```

- atahualpae subsp. atahualpae - Molau says endemic to Bolivia and southeastern Peru, remove -75 point (too far north)
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria atahualpae subsp. atahualpae", lon_min=-74.5, save=true)
```

- vaccinioides - remove spurious ~-7ºS point (too far north)
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria vaccinioides", lat_max=-10.0, save=true)
```

- barbata - remove -74 pt
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria barbata", lon_max=-75, save=true)
```

- bartsiifolia - remove -65 pt
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria bartsiifolia", lon_min=-72, lat_min=-20, save=true)
```

- undulata - remove one -39 point
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria undulata", lat_min=-39, save=true)
```

- revoluta: remove -9 pt
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria revoluta", lat_max=-10, save=true)
```

- boliviana - remove -75 and -66 points, outside range of Northern Bolivia and SE Peru
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria boliviana", lon_min=-74, lon_max=-66, save=true)
```

- glacialis - remove points below -30
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria glacialis", lat_min=-30, save=true)
```

- pinifolia - remove point above -25
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria pinifolia", lat_max=-25, save=true)
```

- engleriana subsp. engleriana - remove -9 point, everything else looks good according to molau
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria engleriana subsp. engleriana", lat_max=-9, save=true)
```

- nudicaulis - remove point above -32
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria nudicaulis", lat_max=-32.5, save=true)
```

- thyrsiflora - remove point above -30
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria thyrsiflora", lat_max=-32, save=true)
```

- heterophylla - remove -50 pt
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria heterophylla", lon_max=-55, save=true)
```

- alba - remove Peru point
```{julia}
filter_countries("Calceolaria alba", ["Peru"])
```

- flexuosa subsp. chrysocalyx - Amazonas Peru point - annotated by Molau as "chrysocalyx Edwin", but monograph uses spec # for luteocalyx
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria flexuosa subsp. chrysocalyx", lat_max=-8, save=true)
```

- germainii - remove outlier point. no digitization for the herbarium sheet, nor any good Argentinian range definition.
```{julia}
filter_coords("Calceolaria germainii", lon_max=-68, save=true)
```

## remove iNat obs./IDs if not from Cocucci/Sersic

from Anahí: "safe" taxa: "uniflora or triandra, or taxa that are invasive like tripartita. Others that you could keep maybe are purpurea, cana, hyssopifolia, fothergillii, boliviana, lanigera, tenella, scapiflora, martinessi, glacialis, nitida, picta"

```{python}
import requests, json, time

headers = {"User-Agent": "calceolaria-scraper/1.0"}
users = ["aacocucci", "aliciasersic"]
users_str = ",".join(users)
all_obs_dict = {}

# Query 1: Observations BY these users
# Query 2: Observations IDENTIFIED BY these users
for role_param in ["user_id", "ident_user_id"]:
    page = 1
    while True:
        r = requests.get("https://api.inaturalist.org/v1/observations",
            headers=headers,
            params={
                role_param: users_str,
                "taxon_id": 129854,      # Calceolaria genus
                "rank": "species",       # only species-level IDs
                "per_page": 200,
                "page": page,
                "order": "desc",
                "order_by": "created_at"
            }
        )

        if r.status_code != 200:
            print(f"Error {r.status_code} for {role_param}: {r.text[:200]}")
            break

        data = r.json()
        results = data.get("results", [])
        total = data.get("total_results", 0)
        print(f"{role_param} | page {page}: got {len(results)} of {total}")

        for obs in results:
            taxon = obs.get("taxon")
            if not taxon:
                continue
            name = taxon.get("name", "")
            
            # ensure it's a species (has a space = genus + epithet)
            if " " not in name:
                continue
            
            obs_id = obs["id"]
            if obs_id not in all_obs_dict:
                all_obs_dict[obs_id] = {
                    "obs_id": obs_id,
                    "observer": obs["user"]["login"],
                    "taxon_name": name,
                    "taxon_id": taxon.get("id"),
                    "date": str(obs.get("created_at", ""))[:10],
                    "quality_grade": obs.get("quality_grade", "none"),
                    "url": f"https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/{obs_id}"
                }

        if len(results) < 200 or page * 200 >= total:
            break
        page += 1
        time.sleep(1)

all_obs = list(all_obs_dict.values())

# Save full data
with open("calceolaria_ids.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(all_obs, f, indent=2)

# Print summary
print(f"\nTotal species-level Calceolaria observations: {len(all_obs)}\n")
print(f"{'Observer':<20} {'Taxon':<40} {'Date':<12} {'Grade':<12} {'Obs ID'}")
print("-" * 100)
for rec in all_obs:
    print(f"{rec['observer']:<20} {rec['taxon_name']:<40} {rec['date']:<12} {rec['quality_grade']:<12} {rec['obs_id']}")
```

```{julia}
# List of safe taxa based on user request
safe_taxa = ["uniflora", "triandra", "tripartita", "purpurea", "cana", "hyssopifolia", "fothergillii", "boliviana", "lanigera", "tenella", "scapiflora", "martinessi", "glacialis", "nitida", "picta"]

# Helper to check if a taxon is "safe"
function is_safe_taxon(taxon::String)
    t_lower = lowercase(taxon)
    for s in safe_taxa
        if occursin(s, t_lower)
            return true
        end
    end
    return false
end

# Extract safe URLs from calceolaria_ids.json using Regex
json_text = read("scripts/calceolaria_ids.json", String)
safe_urls = Set([m.match for m in eachmatch(r"https://www\.inaturalist\.org/observations/\d+", json_text)])

clean_dir = "data/occurrence_data/pt_occs_clean"
filtered_dir = "data/occurrence_data/pt_occs_filtered"

function taxon_stem(fname::String)::String
    base = replace(fname, r"\.csv$" => "")
    base = replace(base, r"_georef_merged$" => "")
    base = replace(base, r"-\d{4}_\d{2}_\d{2}_cleaned$" => "")
    return base
end

all_files = filter(f -> endswith(f, ".csv"), readdir(clean_dir))
taxa = sort(unique(taxon_stem.(all_files)))

for t in taxa
    fpath = resolve_clean_file(t, clean_dir)
    isempty(fpath) && continue

    df = DataFrame(CSV.File(fpath; missingstring=["", "NA"]))
    if nrow(df) == 0
        continue
    end

    if is_safe_taxon(t)
        # Keep everything
        CSV.write(joinpath(filtered_dir, basename(fpath)), df)
    else
        # Remove HUMAN_OBSERVATION unless their occurrenceID is in safe_urls
        keep_mask = map(eachrow(df)) do row
            if row.basisOfRecord == "HUMAN_OBSERVATION"
                !ismissing(row.occurrenceID) && (row.occurrenceID in safe_urls)
            else
                true
            end
        end
        filtered_df = df[keep_mask, :]
        CSV.write(joinpath(filtered_dir, basename(fpath)), filtered_df)
    end
end
```

```{julia}
# Compare the number of points in raw vs filtered
clean_dir = "data/occurrence_data/pt_occs_clean"
filtered_dir = "data/occurrence_data/pt_occs_filtered"

changed_taxa = String[]
summary_rows = []

all_files = filter(f -> endswith(f, ".csv"), readdir(clean_dir))

for fname in all_files
    clean_path = joinpath(clean_dir, fname)
    filtered_path = joinpath(filtered_dir, fname)

    if !isfile(filtered_path)
        continue
    end

    df_clean = DataFrame(CSV.File(clean_path; missingstring=["", "NA"]))
    df_filt = DataFrame(CSV.File(filtered_path; missingstring=["", "NA"]))

    n_clean = nrow(df_clean)
    n_filt = nrow(df_filt)

    t_name = taxon_stem(fname)
    push!(summary_rows, (taxon=t_name, original=n_clean, remaining_total=n_filt, removed=n_clean - n_filt))

    if n_clean > n_filt
        push!(changed_taxa, fname)
    end
end

summary_df = DataFrame(summary_rows)
CSV.write("data/occurrence_data/occ_cleaning_summary.csv", summary_df)
```

```{julia}
# Open a single PDF device to save all the side-by-side plots
R"pdf('data/occurrence_data/filtered_occs_comparison.pdf', width=10, height=5)"

for fname in changed_taxa
    clean_path = joinpath(clean_dir, fname)
    filtered_path = joinpath(filtered_dir, fname)

    df_clean = DataFrame(CSV.File(clean_path; missingstring=["", "NA"]))
    df_filt = DataFrame(CSV.File(filtered_path; missingstring=["", "NA"]))

    # Skip taxa that had fewer than 15 points originally
    if nrow(df_clean) < 15
        continue
    end

    taxon_label = taxon_stem(fname)

    orig_lat = Vector{Union{Missing,Float64}}(df_clean.latitude)
    orig_lon = Vector{Union{Missing,Float64}}(df_clean.longitude)

    filt_lat = Vector{Union{Missing,Float64}}(df_filt.latitude)
    filt_lon = Vector{Union{Missing,Float64}}(df_filt.longitude)

    @rput taxon_label orig_lat orig_lon filt_lat filt_lon

    R"""
    library(ggplot2)
    if (!requireNamespace("gridExtra", quietly = TRUE)) {
      install.packages("gridExtra", repos="http://cran.us.r-project.org")
    }
    library(gridExtra)

    orig_pts <- data.frame(latitude = as.numeric(orig_lat), longitude = as.numeric(orig_lon))
    filt_pts <- data.frame(latitude = as.numeric(filt_lat), longitude = as.numeric(filt_lon))

    all_lat <- orig_pts$latitude
    all_lon <- orig_pts$longitude

    world   <- annotation_borders(database="world", colour="gray80", fill="gray80")
    borders <- annotation_borders(database="world", colour="gray40", fill=NA, linewidth=0.5)

    xlims <- c(min(all_lon, na.rm=TRUE)-2, max(all_lon, na.rm=TRUE)+2)
    ylims <- c(min(all_lat, na.rm=TRUE)-2, max(all_lat, na.rm=TRUE)+2)

    p1 <- ggplot() + world + borders +
        geom_point(data=orig_pts, aes(x=longitude, y=latitude), color="blue", size=2, alpha=0.7) +
        coord_sf(xlim=xlims, ylim=ylims) +
        labs(title=paste0("Original (", nrow(orig_pts), ")"), x="Longitude", y="Latitude") +
        theme_minimal() + theme(plot.title=element_text(size=10))
        
    p2 <- ggplot() + world + borders +
        geom_point(data=filt_pts, aes(x=longitude, y=latitude), color="darkgreen", size=2, alpha=0.7) +
        coord_sf(xlim=xlims, ylim=ylims) +
        labs(title=paste0("Filtered (", nrow(filt_pts), ")"), x="Longitude", y="Latitude") +
        theme_minimal() + theme(plot.title=element_text(size=10))
        
    p_combined <- grid.arrange(p1, p2, ncol=2, top=taxon_label)
    print(p_combined)
    """
end

R"dev.off()"
println("Plots saved to data/occurrence_data/filtered_occs_comparison.pdf")
```

```{julia}
plot_all_occurrence_maps()
plot_all_occurrence_maps(only_clean=true, pdf_file="data/occurrence_data/occurrence_maps.pdf")
```

## fixits

- edge cases: not research grade, but id'ed by cocucci/sersic?
- edge cases for members in my `homonyms.csv` file --> 
```{julia}
homonymsa = intersect(taxa_traits.scientificName, homonyms.scientificName)
homonymsb = intersect(taxa_traits.scientificName, homonyms.validName)
homonyms_indataset = union(homonymsa, homonymsb)
```

there's 13 of these, we'll need to be extra careful and double check:
  1. occurrences of the synonym/homonym without species authority
  2. look at overlap of range between synonyms
(make sure nivalis Puppo subsp. gets removed from ascendens)

- taxa that could be merged at infraspecific level and brought up above 15+ pts:
    - Calceolaria_meyeniana_subsp._meyeniana (we didn't pull just meyeniana but could do that?)
    - Calceolaria_incarum_subsp._incarum (same as above)
    - Calceolaria_arachnoidea_subsp._arachnoidea (same as above)
    - Calceolaria_glandulosa_subsp._alicahuensis could be merged with Calceolaria_glandulosa_subsp._glandulosa
    - Calceolaria_flexuosa_subsp._flexuosa could be merged with Calceolaria_flexuosa_subsp._chrysocalyx
    - Calceolaria_engleriana_subsp._lutea could be merged with Calceolaria_engleriana_subsp._engleriana
    - We have 6 Calceolaria_corymbosa subsp. --> ask Anahí about divergence / consult with Ehrhart?

- after georeferencing, figure out reporting back to herbaria?
